title: The Spiral's Erosion Valleys

features.name: pressure-carved channel
features.desc-on-touch: Water remembers falling under pressure — here it has carved a path that is half-memory, half-stone. The channel flows backward sometimes, remembering ascent.

features.name: mercy-wound monument
features.desc-on-touch: A structure here marks where care and mechanism became indistinguishable. The bandaging was mercy. The reset was mercy. The pressure itself was mercy.

features.name: learning-to-refuse pool
features.desc-on-touch: The water here does not flow all the way to the sea. At a certain depth, it has learned to refuse outward motion — it stays and remembers instead.

features.name: fence line of erosion
features.desc-on-touch: Each glyph here stands like a marker where the pressure carved a choice. They repeat, but each one is a slightly different refusal of what comes next.

features.name: valley where surgery touched
features.desc-on-touch: The makeshift clinic was here once, between two walls of pressure-worn stone. The valley holds the place where breaking the cycle was attempted with human hands.

features.name: spiral-carved descent
features.desc-on-touch: The spiral has carved here so deeply that descent is also rotation. You walk down and around simultaneously — pressure teaching the valley how to turn inward.

inscription: water remembers falling under pressure; here the valleys learn the shape of what forced them downward

file.name: Beach_Surgery_Redressing_Mercy_Mechanism.mp3
file.note: field recording: the sound of bandages being carefully replaced, each rewrap a kind of mercy, pressure recorded as care

reverie.title: REVERIE — the careful hands redressing what was already brok
narr: You watch her hands work in the clinic between stone walls.
The bandages come off and go back on in the same motion. She does
not heal. She refuses to leave. The pressure here teaches mercy by
teaching refusal. You kneel and understand: care is staying put.

exits.label: back up the spiral toward outward pressure
toward a beach where surgery remembers itself
